During an online meeting, display the QR code via screen share and convey the password verbally. For external recipients, send it to a pre-agreed contact address through a separate message. This kind of channel separation is a high-impact measure for relatively little extra effort. Also, avoid passwords that can be guessed from the document name or company name, and steer clear of words that are too short.

When sharing with external partner companies or temporary team members in particular, make it explicit whether re-sharing is allowed. Handing over a file also means handing over responsibility for how it's managed afterward.
安全な在宅勤務は、手順を複雑にしすぎないことから始まる: Safe Remote Work Starts with Keeping Procedures Simple
複数名へ送る場合は、個人名を宛先に並べるだけでなく、共有の目的も明記します。「A: When sending to multiple people, do more than just list the recipients' names — also state the purpose of the share clearly. Labels like "Estimate review for Company A," "Candidate documents for recruitment interview," or "Invoice for accounting review" help recipients immediately confirm whether the file was intended for them. Preventing misdirected sends means giving recipients enough context to catch a mistake, not just relying on the sender to get it right.
機密ファイルを扱う業務では、後から「誰に、いつ、何を渡したか」を確認できることも: When working with confidential files, being able to look back and confirm who received what and when is equally important. Even without detailed audit logs, simply leaving a sharing record in a chat tool or task manager makes it much easier to investigate any questions or issues that arise. A record doesn't need to be elaborate — the file name, recipient, purpose, expiry, and whether receipt was confirmed is enough.
機密ファイルの共有で避けたいのは、必要な作業が終わった後もリンクが生き続ける状態: One situation to avoid when sharing confidential files is leaving a link active after the work it was created for is done. A long-lived link in a chat history can be accessed by someone stumbling across it later, forwarded unintentionally, or used to reference an outdated version of a file. When using a temporary sharing service like FS!QR, set the expiry to cover only the time the task actually requires.

ただし、記録に機密情報そのものを貼り付ける必要はありません。ファイルの中身ではな: That said, there's no need to include the confidential content itself in the log. The goal is to record the fact that a share took place, not what the file contained. Keep the log to the minimum necessary, and keep the file itself short-lived through temporary sharing — this combination is what makes it possible to work both safely and efficiently from home.
機密ファイルを共有するとき、パスワードを設定しても、共有URLと同じメッセージに: When sharing a confidential file, setting a password only helps if that password isn't sent in the same message as the sharing URL. If someone gains access to the same chat thread or email, both the URL and password are exposed at once. Ideally, the sharing URL and password should always be communicated through separate channels.

Even simple habits, applied consistently every time, add up to meaningful security.
メールやチャットでよくある事故は、アップロード後の共有リンクを間違った相手に送っ: One of the most common accidents in email and chat is sending a shared link to the wrong person after uploading. Because anyone who has the link can often access it, always confirming the recipient is non-negotiable. Before uploading a file, verify who you're sharing it with, whether they are internal or external, and whether they genuinely have the right to view it.
ルール4：ローカル保存と再共有の扱いを決める: Rule 4: Set Clear Rules for Local Saving and Re-Sharing
社外公開済みの案内資料なら、短時間のURL共有だけで十分な場合があります。一方、: For documents that are already publicly available, a brief URL share may be all that's needed. Files containing customer names, amounts, addresses, or non-public information, on the other hand, should combine password protection, recipient verification, and a shorter expiry. To avoid second-guessing every time you share, agree on a few simple team guidelines — for example, "always use a password for files containing personal information" or "end the share after the contract has been acknowledged" — and things become much easier to manage consistently.
ルール5：共有期限を短くし、受け取り確認を必ず取る: Rule 5: Keep Sharing Windows Short and Always Confirm Receipt
すべてのファイルを同じ扱いにすると、重要な資料に対して保護が足りなかったり、逆に: Treating every file the same leads to two problems: insufficient protection for sensitive materials, and unnecessary overhead for routine ones. Start by classifying the files you share into a few basic sensitivity levels. Publicly available documents, internal-only materials, files containing personal data or commercial terms, and documents related to contracts or HR each call for a different level of protection.

自宅は仕事場であり、同時に管理外の環境でもある: Home Is Both Your Workplace and an Uncontrolled Environment
在宅勤務では、受け取ったファイルがダウンロードフォルダに残り続けることがあります: When working from home, received files can linger in a download folder indefinitely. Even if the sharing service automatically deletes the file on its end, it cannot remove copies saved on the recipient's device. This is why, when sharing confidential files, you should always communicate instructions for handling the file after it has been received.
セキュリティ対策は、厳しくしすぎると現場で回らなくなります。毎回複雑な申請が必要: Security measures that are too burdensome simply won't be followed in practice. If every share requires a complex approval process, or if recipients need to install a dedicated app, people will reach for easier alternatives when time is short. That's exactly why it's important to keep the fundamentals — browser-based temporary sharing, password protection, short expiry windows, and receipt confirmation — in a form that anyone on the team can follow without friction.
期限を短くする代わりに、受け取り確認を確実に行います。相手がダウンロードできたか: Rather than leaving a file available indefinitely, keep the sharing window short and confirm receipt actively. Once you know the other party has successfully downloaded, opened, and confirmed the right file, there's no reason to leave it accessible any longer. Agree on a closing condition in advance: until the meeting ends for in-meeting document review, end of day for same-day reviews, or until acknowledgement of receipt for temporary sends to clients.

What matters is breaking down the risks before transferring a file and turning your approach into concrete, repeatable rules. ルール1：機密度を分けて共有方法を変える: Rule 1: Match Your Sharing Method to the Sensitivity Level
在宅勤務が日常化すると、顧客情報、見積書、契約書、勤怠データ、設計資料など、機密: As remote work becomes the norm, more and more situations arise where highly sensitive files — customer information, estimates, contracts, attendance records, design documents — need to be handled from home. In a company office, there's a degree of control over the network, printers, storage, and who can see your screen. At home, many of those controls disappear: family members share the space, you're on a personal network, devices get carried around, and it's easy to leave a screen unattended.
